dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3477 IS: Support Our Troops Shipping Relief Act of 2025
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Blumenthal introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs

form.official-title: To exempt humanitarian care packages sent to members of the Armed Forces stationed overseas from certain postal reporting requirements, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Support Our Troops Shipping Relief Act of 2025.

section.header: Treatment by United States Postal Service of humanitarian care packages sent to members of the Armed Forces stationed overseas

subsection.text: Chapter 34 of title 39, United States Code, is amended by inserting after section 3401 the following:

section.header: Treatment of humanitarian care packages sent to members of the Armed Forces stationed overseas

paragraph.text: The term Armed Forces has the meaning given that term in section 101(a) of title 10.
The term covered shipment means a shipment that—

subparagraph.text: is addressed to a military mail address or other destination authorized by the Secretary of Defense; and
consists of one or more humanitarian care packages.

paragraph.text: The term humanitarian care package means a parcel containing donated goods intended solely for the comfort, welfare, or morale of members of the Armed Forces at duty stations overseas.

The term military mail address means an Army Post Office (APO), Fleet Post Office (FPO), or Diplomatic Post Office (DPO) address designated by the Secretary of Defense for receipt of mail by members of the Armed Forces stationed overseas.
The Postal Service shall treat any covered shipment as domestic mail for all purposes, regardless of destination.
Not later than 30 days after the date of enactment of the Support Our Troops Shipping Relief Act of 2025, the Postmaster General shall prescribe regulations to carry out paragraph (1).

In implementing paragraph (1), to the extent that the Postal Service requires a customs declaration form for covered shipments, the Postal Service shall accept a simplified customs declaration form, which may list categories of contents (such as snack foods, personal hygiene items, letters and cards, or similar general descriptions) instead of the classification under the Harmonized System or country-of-origin information for each article in the shipment.

subsection.text: The table of sections for chapter 34 of title 39, United States Code, is amended by adding at the end the following:

toc.toc-entry: 3402. Treatment of humanitarian care packages sent to members of the Armed Forces stationed overseas.
